vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or non-viable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable structure, though it appears severely compromised.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos structure visible, though health is critical.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver foliage.

plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows severe dehydration and potential necrosis. The white rabbit anchor is present but the plant is failing to thrive.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, crystalline or shell-like fragments on the soil surface of P3, likely an intervention or mineral buildup.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Minimal change; persistent lack of vegetative growth across all pots except for the static P3 structure.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No observable growth or recovery; the biome remains in a state of stasis or decline.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total failure. P3 is the only surviving occupant, and it is showing signs of extreme stress. The absence of P1, P2, and P4 suggests either total mortality or complete removal of the specimens.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white, fragmented material in P3 is a persistent anomaly. The lack of any green foliage in P1, P2, and P4 is a significant deviation from the registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image stack. My first action was to establish the spatial orientation of the pots and verify the presence of the scale anchor (white rabbit). Upon validation, I compared the visual data against the registry. The findings are grim: the biome is largely devoid of the expected flora. P3 remains, but its health is poor. The other pots appear to be empty soil, indicating systemic loss. No new growth has occurred during the observation window.
